World 06 · 1 state

Purpose & Direction

Films for the search that doesn’t end on a map — the long, restless walk toward meaning, vocation, or the thing you can’t name yet.

Choose an emotion
Feeling Lost in Life
On Feeling Lost in Life

You did what you were supposed to do. You followed the plan, or you abandoned the plan, or you never had a plan — and here you are, in a life that does not quite feel like yours. The compass stopped working. Other people seem to know where they are going, which mostly means they are better at performing certainty. These films are for the season of not-knowing — not as a failure state to escape but as a territory worth understanding. Some of the most important living happens in the lost years. These filmmakers knew that.

Feeling Lost in Life

Films for the season when nothing seems to point anywhere.

CS8.4
2007

Into the Wild

Sean Penn

View Film →

A young man abandons his possessions, savings, and family after graduating, hitchhiking toward Alaska in search of an honest life. Based on Christopher McCandless’s true story.

CS9.0
2020

Soul

Pete Docter

View Film →

A middle-school music teacher who finally lands the gig of his dreams dies on the same day — and finds himself in a celestial waystation, trying to get back.

CS7.9
2013

The Secret Life of Walter Mitty

Ben Stiller

View Film →

A daydreaming photo archivist at LIFE magazine, faced with losing his job, finally steps outside his fantasies and chases down one missing negative across the world.

CS9.3
2023

Perfect Days

Wim Wenders

View Film →

A Tokyo public-toilet cleaner lives a routine of cassette tapes, paperbacks, and small kindnesses. The film follows him quietly through several days.

CS9.4
1952

Ikiru

Akira Kurosawa

View Film →

A Tokyo bureaucrat learns he has months to live and quietly devotes the time he has left to building one small park in a neglected corner of his city.

CS8.8
2020

Nomadland

Chloé Zhao

View Film →

After losing her job and her husband, a woman in her sixties packs her van and becomes a modern nomad, moving from gig to gig through the American West.

CS8.2
2014

Wild

Jean-Marc Vallée

View Film →

After losing her mother and her marriage, a woman with no hiking experience walks the 1,100-mile Pacific Crest Trail alone. Based on Cheryl Strayed’s memoir.

CS7.9
2004

Garden State

Zach Braff

View Film →

A medicated young actor returns to his New Jersey hometown for his mother’s funeral and meets a girl who refuses to let him stay numb.

CS8.6
2012

Frances Ha

Noah Baumbach

View Film →

A twenty-seven-year-old aspiring dancer in New York drifts between apartments, friendships, and ideas of herself, trying to land somewhere.

CS8.7
2016

Paterson

Jim Jarmusch

View Film →

A bus driver in Paterson, New Jersey, named Paterson, writes poems between routes. The film follows seven days of his quiet, devoted life.

CS9.0
2001

Y Tu Mamá También

Alfonso Cuarón

View Film →

Two teenage boys and an older woman road-trip to a mythical beach in Mexico. The journey changes all three.

CS8.8
2004

Sideways

Alexander Payne

View Film →

A depressed, divorced writer and his soon-to-be-married friend drive through California wine country. The trip tests both their ideas of themselves.

CS8.1
2007

The Darjeeling Limited

Wes Anderson

View Film →

Three brothers travel by train across India a year after their father’s death, trying to rediscover their relationship.

CS8.1
2013

Tracks

John Curran

View Film →

Robyn Davidson walks 1,700 miles across the Australian outback alone with four camels. Based on her memoir.

CS8.5
2002

About a Boy

Chris and Paul Weitz

View Film →

A wealthy, permanently idle London man lives entirely on royalties and avoids all adult responsibility until a twelve-year-old forces him to grow up.

CS8.7
2004

The Motorcycle Diaries

Walter Salles

View Film →

A young Ernesto Guevara and his friend travel through South America on a motorcycle. The journey changes who Guevara becomes.

CS7.7
2012

Liberal Arts

Josh Radnor

View Film →

A thirty-five-year-old returns to his college campus for a professor’s retirement and befriends a nineteen-year-old who seems more certain of things than he is.

CS7.3
2010

Eat Pray Love

Ryan Murphy

View Film →

After a divorce, a woman leaves New York to spend a year in Italy, India, and Bali looking for herself.

CS9.1
2008

Departures

Yōjirō Takita

View Film →

A failed cellist in Tokyo accidentally becomes an encoffiner — a person who prepares the dead for burial — and finds unexpected meaning.

CS8.8
2009

A Single Man

Tom Ford

View Film →

A British professor in 1962 Los Angeles has decided today will be his last. The film is what the day teaches him.

CS8.3
2016

Captain Fantastic

Matt Ross

View Film →

A father raises his children off-grid before the outside world intrudes. The family must decide what to keep.

CS7.8
2010

The Way

Emilio Estevez

View Film →

A California doctor walks the Camino de Santiago after his son dies on the first day of the pilgrimage.

CS7.7
2014

Laggies

Lynn Shelton

View Film →

A twenty-eight-year-old who still lives like a teenager runs away from a marriage proposal and hides in a teenager’s house.

CS8.6
1984

Stranger Than Paradise

Jim Jarmusch

View Film →

A Hungarian immigrant in New York, his American cousin, and his friend drive aimlessly from New York to Cleveland to Florida.

Next world · 07
Existential Confusion →

For the question that hasn’t shut up since you turned twenty.

Films don't just entertain. They quietly confirm you were seen — long before anyone in your real life knew how to.

18 emotional states·277 curated films·Growing weekly
